query: To fix the security vulnerability where scheduled jobs can execute arbitrary Java methods via the invoke target, what is the minimal set of code changes needed while still preserving the scheduling functionality?

When a role's permissions are modified, automatically refresh the permissions of all currently online users who hold that role, so that permission changes take effect immediately without requiring users to log out and back in.
Fix the issue where the @Sensitive data desensitization (masking) annotation is not taking effect during JSON serialization, tracked as issue IIPBZR.
Add a drawer-style detail panel to the user list page that shows detailed user information when clicking on a user, replacing or supplementing the existing dialog approach.
Add batch sorting save functionality to the menu management module, allowing administrators to reorder menu items and save all order changes at once instead of updating one at a time.
Optimize the scheduled task detail page display by creating a dedicated detail view component, and supplement the task log with an execution time field to show how long each job execution took.

Add a notification and announcement message reminder feature to the homepage, including a bell icon in the navigation bar, a read-tracking system for notices, and a dropdown panel showing unread notifications.
